_about: Phase 2 demo graph for the Flow B queued-alert pattern (deferred from Phase 1, now possible with pack_event / queue_events / generic unpack_event). Subscription and redemption events are packed into a record, queued FIFO, and released one at a time on a clock tick so overlapping alerts don't stomp each other. Each released alert spawns an ephemeral overlay clip and sets its text. Before importing: fill in the TODO placeholder ids in defaultConfig (overlive account ids, template clip id). Import via PUT /api/graphs/:id with the descriptor field set to this JSON object minus the leading underscore-prefixed metadata keys.
_setup: 1. Create a compose_layer (or scene_node) to act as the alert overlay TEMPLATE. Mark it hidden — spawns are auto-unhidden. If using text, make it a text_canvas/text_troika node.
2. Author a track_clip owned by that template (mode='relative' or 'override', loop=false, a short duration e.g. 3s) that animates the alert in and out (opacity/position).
3. Hook up Twitch + StreamElements (or one) in TopBar > Overlive Accounts so subscription/redemption events flow.

4. Fill in the TODO defaultConfig values: account on the overlive_subscription and overlive_redemption nodes, clipId on spawn_clip, and tune the clock hz (default 0.5 = release one queued alert every 2s).

5. pack_event's input fields are defined by config.fields (already set to username + message below); wire each overlive output into the matching named input port. unpack_event will expose one output per packed field once the pack→queue→unpack chain is wired.
6. Create a project-scoped graph (POST /api/projects/:projectId/graphs) and PUT this descriptor (minus the _* meta keys) into its descriptor field.
7. Trigger a sub/redemption; alerts queue and pop one per clock tick, each spawning the template clip and setting its text.
label: Sub/Redemption → Queued Alerts
